title: Phoenix API-only project scaffold with PostgreSQL, binary UUIDs, Oban, Req, Cloak

story.i_want_to: scaffold a Phoenix API-only project with PostgreSQL, binary UUIDs as the default primary key type, Oban for background jobs, Req for HTTP client, and Cloak for field-level encryption
story.so_that: all downstream epics build on a consistent, production-ready foundation with the correct libraries pre-configured and no LiveView overhead

rationale: loopctl is an agent-native REST API with no human UI. The scaffold must enforce API-only mode from the start to prevent accidental LiveView dependencies. Binary UUIDs prevent sequential ID guessing, Oban handles webhook delivery and cleanup jobs, Req is the HTTP client for outbound calls, and Cloak provides AES-256-GCM encryption for secrets at rest.

acceptance_criteria.description: Phoenix project is generated with --no-html --no-live --no-mailer --no-dashboard flags; no LiveView, HTML, or mailer dependencies exist in mix.exs
PostgreSQL is configured as the database adapter in config/dev.exs, config/test.exs, and config/runtime.exs with database name loopctl_dev / loopctl_test
Ecto migration generator defaults to binary_id (UUIDv4) for primary keys and foreign keys project-wide via config :loopctl, Loopctl.Repo, migration_primary_key: [type: :binary_id]
Oban is added to mix.exs, configured in config files with a PostgreSQL queue backend, and started in the application supervision tree
Req is added to mix.exs as the HTTP client dependency
Cloak and Cloak.Ecto are added to mix.exs; a Loopctl.Vault module is defined with AES-256-GCM cipher configuration; the encryption key is sourced from a runtime environment variable (CLOAK_KEY)
The application starts successfully with mix phx.server and responds to HTTP requests on the configured port
mix test runs with zero failures on a fresh clone after mix setup
The router mounts a /api/v1 scope with the :api pipeline (JSON accepts, no CSRF)
Jason is configured as the JSON library for both Phoenix and Ecto

Application MUST configure structured JSON logging with Logger backends. Every log entry MUST include tenant_id and request_id metadata when available. Use Logger.metadata/1 to set context in the auth pipeline.

Application MUST define Telemetry events for key operations: API request duration, story status transitions, webhook deliveries, audit log writes. Use :telemetry.execute/3 with measurements and metadata.

The application MUST include the RemoteIp plug (or equivalent) in the endpoint pipeline to correctly resolve client IP addresses when deployed behind a reverse proxy (nginx). This is required for IP-based rate limiting on unauthenticated endpoints (registration, health check).

test_cases.name: Application boots without errors
expected_results: No error logs are emitted during startup

test_cases.name: Oban is running in the supervision tree
steps: Check that Oban process is alive via Process.whereis(Oban)
expected_results: Oban process is running and alive

test_cases.name: Repo generates binary UUIDs for primary keys
preconditions: A test schema with a primary key is defined
steps: Inspect the id field of the returned struct
expected_results: The id is a valid UUID string (matches UUID format regex)
The id is not a sequential integer

test_cases.name: Cloak Vault encrypts and decrypts a value
expected_results: Encrypted value is a binary different from "test_secret"

test_cases.name: API pipeline returns JSON content type
steps: Send GET request to /api/v1 (or any mounted route)

test_cases.name: No LiveView or HTML dependencies present
steps: Check mix.exs for phoenix_live_view, phoenix_html, phoenix_live_dashboard dependencies
Check for any *_html.ex or *_live.ex modules in lib/
expected_results: No LiveView, HTML, or Dashboard dependencies exist in mix.exs
No HTML or LiveView modules exist in the lib/ directory

test_cases.name: Req is available as an HTTP client
expected_results: Returns true, confirming Req module is available

technical_notes: Set config :loopctl, Loopctl.Repo, migration_primary_key: [type: :binary_id], migration_foreign_key: [type: :binary_id] in config/config.exs
Oban config: queues: [default: 10, webhooks: 5, cleanup: 2] — adjust as needed in later epics
Cloak key should be 32-byte base64-encoded, generated via :crypto.strong_rand_bytes(32) |> Base.encode64()
Loopctl.Vault must be added to the application supervision tree before Repo
Ensure config/test.exs sets Oban to testing mode: config :loopctl, Oban, testing: :inline

Testing infrastructure: (1) Mox mocks defined in test/support/mocks.ex, (2) Default permissive stubs in DataCase/ConnCase setup via Mox.stub/3, (3) Mox.set_mox_from_context(tags) for async isolation, (4) setup :verify_on_exit! in every test module, (5) Fixtures in test/support/fixtures.ex with build/2 (no DB) and fixture/2 (DB insert + auto-deps), (6) NEVER Application.put_env in tests.
